256→open class SoundPlayer {
257→    val currentAudioUri: String?
258→        get() = mCurrentAudioUri?.toString()
259→
260→    val isCurrentAudioFinished: Boolean
261→        get() = mMediaPlayer == null
262→
263→    /**
264→     * Media player used to play the sounds. It's Nullable and that it is set only if a sound is playing or paused, otherwise it is null.
265→     */
266→    private var mMediaPlayer: MediaPlayer? = null
267→
268→    private var mCurrentAudioUri: Uri? = null
269→
270→    /**
271→     * AudioManager to request/release audio focus
272→     */
273→    private var mAudioManager: AudioManager? = null
274→
275→    private var mAudioFocusRequest: AudioFocusRequest? = null
276→
277→    /**
278→     * Plays the given sound or video and sets playAllListener if available on media player to start next media.
279→     * If videoView is null and the media is a video, then a request is sent to start the VideoPlayer Activity
280→     */
281→    fun playSound(
282→        soundPath: String,
283→        onCompletionListener: OnCompletionListener?,
284→        errorListener: Sound.OnErrorListener?
285→    ) {
286→        Timber.d("Playing single sound")
287→        val completionListener = onCompletionListener ?: SingleSoundCompletionListener()
288→        val errorHandler = errorListener
289→            ?: Sound.OnErrorListener { _: MediaPlayer?, what: Int, extra: Int, _: String? ->
290→                Timber.w("Media Error: (%d, %d). Calling OnCompletionListener", what, extra)
291→                CONTINUE_AUDIO
292→            }
293→        playSoundInternal(soundPath, completionListener, errorHandler)
294→    }
295→
296→    /**
297→     * Plays a sound without ensuring that the playAllListener will release the audio
298→     */
299→    private fun playSoundInternal(
300→        soundPath: String,
301→        completionListener: OnCompletionListener,
302→        errorHandler: Sound.OnErrorListener
303→    ) {
304→        Timber.d("Playing %s", soundPath)
305→        val soundUri = Uri.parse(soundPath)
306→        mCurrentAudioUri = soundUri
307→
308→        val context = AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext
309→
310→        // Play media
311→        fun playMedia() {
312→            try {
313→                // Create media player
314→                if (mMediaPlayer == null) {
315→                    Timber.d("Creating media player for playback")
316→                    mMediaPlayer = MediaPlayer()
317→                } else {
318→                    Timber.d("Resetting media for playback")
319→                    mMediaPlayer!!.reset()
320→                }
321→                val mediaPlayer = mMediaPlayer!!
322→                mAudioManager =
323→                    mAudioManager ?: context.getSystemService(Context.AUDIO_SERVICE) as AudioManager
324→
325→                mediaPlayer.setOnErrorListener { mp: MediaPlayer?, which: Int, extra: Int ->
326→                    val errorHandling = errorHandler.onError(
327→                        mp,
328→                        which,
329→                        extra,
330→                        soundPath
331→                    )
332→                    // returning false calls onComplete()
333→                    return@setOnErrorListener when (errorHandling) {
334→                        CONTINUE_AUDIO -> false
335→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> {
336→                            playMedia()
337→                            true
338→                        }
339→                        Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.STOP_AUDIO -> {
340→                            stopSounds()
341→                            true
342→                        }
343→                    }
344→                }
345→                // Setup the MediaPlayer
346→                mediaPlayer.setDataSource(context, soundUri)
347→                mediaPlayer.setAudioAttributes(
348→                    AudioAttributes.Builder()
349→                        .setContentType(AudioAttributes.CONTENT_TYPE_MUSIC)
350→                        .build()
351→                )
352→                mediaPlayer.setOnPreparedListener {
353→                    Timber.d("Starting media player")
354→                    it.start()
355→                }
356→                mediaPlayer.setOnCompletionListener(completionListener)
357→                mediaPlayer.prepareAsync()
358→                Timber.d("Requesting audio focus")
359→
360→                // Set mAudioFocusRequest for API 26 and above.
361→                if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
362→                    mAudioFocusRequest =
363→                        AudioFocusRequest.Builder(AudioManager.AUDIOFOCUS_GAIN_TRANSIENT_MAY_DUCK)
364→                            .setOnAudioFocusChangeListener(audioFocusChangeListener)
365→                            .build()
366→                }
367→                CompatHelper.compat.requestAudioFocus(
368→                    mAudioManager!!,
369→                    audioFocusChangeListener,
370→                    mAudioFocusRequest
371→                )
372→            } catch (e: Exception) {
373→                Timber.e(e, "playSounds - Error reproducing sound %s", soundPath)
374→                when (
375→                    errorHandler.onError(
376→                        mMediaPlayer,
377→                        MediaPlayer.MEDIA_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED,
378→                        0,
379→                        soundPath
380→                    )
381→                ) {
382→                    CONTINUE_AUDIO -> {
383→                        Timber.d("Force playing next sound.")
384→                        completionListener.onCompletion(mMediaPlayer)
385→                    }
386→                    Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.STOP_AUDIO -> stopSounds()
387→                    Sound.OnErrorListener.ErrorHandling.RETRY_AUDIO -> playMedia()
388→                }
389→            }
390→        }
391→
392→        playMedia()
393→    }
394→
395→    /**
396→     * Releases the sound.
397→     */
398→    private fun releaseSound() {
399→        Timber.d("Releasing sounds and abandoning audio focus")
400→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
401→            // Required to remove warning: "mediaplayer went away with unhandled events"
402→            // https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9609479/android-mediaplayer-went-away-with-unhandled-events
403→            it.reset()
404→            it.release()
405→            mMediaPlayer = null
406→        }
407→        mAudioManager?.let {
408→            // mAudioFocusRequest was initialised for API 26 and above in playSoundInternal().
409→            CompatHelper.compat.abandonAudioFocus(it, audioFocusChangeListener, mAudioFocusRequest)
410→            mAudioManager = null
411→        }
412→    }
413→
414→    open fun stopSounds() {
415→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
416→            it.stop()
417→            // TODO: Inefficient. Determine whether we want to release or stop, don't do both
418→            // Ensure `currentAudioUri` etc... still work when we do this
419→            releaseSound()
420→        }
421→    }
422→
423→    fun playOrPauseSound() {
424→        mMediaPlayer?.let {
425→            if (it.isPlaying) {
426→                it.pause()
427→            } else {
428→                it.start()
429→            }
430→        }
431→    }
432→
433→    /** #5414 - Ensures playing a single sound performs cleanup  */
434→    private inner class SingleSoundCompletionListener : OnCompletionListener {
435→        override fun onCompletion(mp: MediaPlayer) {
436→            Timber.d("Single Sound completed")
437→            releaseSound()
438→        }
439→    }
440→
441→    companion object {
442→        /**
443→         * Listener to handle audio focus. Currently blank because we're not respecting losing focus from other apps.
444→         */
445→        private val audioFocusChangeListener = OnAudioFocusChangeListener { }
446→    }
447→}

449→@Throws(Exception::class)
450→private fun MediaMetadataRetriever.getDuration(context: Context, uri: Uri): Long {
451→    this.setDataSource(context, uri)
452→    val duration = this.extractMetadata(MediaMetadataRetriever.METADATA_KEY_DURATION)
453→    return duration!!.toLong()
454→}

 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analyticsOptIn"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }

110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }

117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }

160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }

183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }

265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }

279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }

297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }

24→
25→fun SharedPreferences.get(key: String): Any? {
26→    return all[key]
27→}
28→
29→/**
30→ * Sets the callback to be invoked when this preference is changed by the user
31→ * (but before the internal state has been updated) on the internal onPreferenceChangeListener,
32→ * returning true on it by default
33→ * @param onPreferenceChangeListener The callback to be invoked
34→ */
35→fun Preference.setOnPreferenceChangeListener(onPreferenceChangeListener: (newValue: Any) -> Unit) {
36→    this.setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newValue ->
37→        onPreferenceChangeListener(newValue)
38→        true
39→    }
40→}
41→
42→/** Obtains a non-null reference to the preference defined by the key, or throws  */
43→inline fun <reified T : Preference> PreferenceFragmentCompat.requirePreference(key: String): T {
44→    val preference = findPreference<Preference>(key)
45→        ?: throw IllegalStateException("missing preference: '$key'")
46→    return preference as T
47→}
48→
49→/**
50→ * Obtains a non-null reference to the preference whose
51→ * key is defined with given [resId] or throws
52→ * e.g. `requirePreference(R.string.day_theme_key)` returns
53→ * the preference whose key is `@string/day_theme_key`
54→ * The resource IDs with preferences keys can be found on `res/values/preferences.xml`
55→ */
56→inline fun <reified T : Preference> PreferenceFragmentCompat.requirePreference(@StringRes resId: Int): T {
57→    val key = getString(resId)
58→    return requirePreference(key)
59→}
60→
61→/** shorthand method to get the default [SharedPreferences] instance */
62→fun Context.sharedPrefs(): SharedPreferences {
63→    return PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(this)
64→}

            android:icon="@drawable/ic_color_lens_white_24dp"
            app:useSimpleSummaryProvider="true"/>
        <ListPreference
            android:defaultValue="1"
            android:entries="@array/day_theme_labels"
            android:entryValues="@array/day_theme_values"
            android:key="@string/day_theme_key"
            android:shouldDisableView="true"
            android:title="@string/day_theme"
            android:icon="@drawable/ic_light_mode_24"
            app:useSimpleSummaryProvider="true"
            search:ignore="true"/>
        <ListPreference
            android:defaultValue="3"
            android:entries="@array/night_theme_labels"
            android:entryValues="@array/night_theme_values"
            android:key="@string/night_theme_key"
            android:shouldDisableView="true"
            android:title="@string/night_theme"
            android:icon="@drawable/ic_dark_mode_24"

        <ListPreference
            android:defaultValue="0"
            android:key="@string/fullscreen_mode_preference"
            android:entries="@array/full_screen_mode_labels"

            android:summary="@string/vertical_centering_summ"
            android:title="@string/vertical_centering" />
        <SwitchPreferenceCompat
            android:key="@string/show_estimates_preference"
            android:summary="@string/show_estimates_summ"
            android:title="@string/show_estimates" />
        <ListPreference

            android:entryValues="@array/answer_buttons_position"
            android:key="@string/answer_buttons_position_preference"
            android:title="@string/answer_buttons_position"
            app:useSimpleSummaryProvider="true"/>
        <SwitchPreferenceCompat
            android:key="@string/show_topbar_preference"
            android:defaultValue="true"
            android:summary="@string/show_top_bar_summ"
            android:title="@string/show_top_bar" />
        <SwitchPreferenceCompat
            android:key="@string/show_progress_preference"
            android:dependency="@string/show_topbar_preference"
            android:summary="@string/show_progress_summ"
            android:title="@string/show_progress" />
        <SwitchPreferenceCompat

        <SwitchPreferenceCompat

            />
        <SwitchPreferenceCompat

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.res.Resources
 21→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 22→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 24→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 25→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 26→import timber.log.Timber
 27→import java.io.BufferedReader
 28→import java.io.IOException
 29→import java.io.InputStream
 30→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 31→
 32→class HtmlGenerator(
 33→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 34→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 35→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 36→    val resources: Resources
 37→) {
 38→
 39→    @CheckResult
 40→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 41→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 45→        return when (side) {
 46→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 47→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 52→        return Sound.expandSounds(content)
 53→    }
 54→
 55→    companion object {
 56→        fun createInstance(
 57→            context: Context,
 58→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 59→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 60→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 61→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 62→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 63→
 64→            return HtmlGenerator(
 65→                typeAnswer,
 66→                cardAppearance,
 67→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 68→                context.resources
 69→            )
 70→        }
 71→
 72→        /**
 73→         * Load the template for the card
 74→         */
 75→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 76→            try {
 77→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 78→                return CardTemplate(data)
 79→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 80→                Timber.w(e)
 81→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 82→            }
 83→        }
 84→
 85→        /**
 86→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 87→         *
 88→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 89→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 90→         */
 91→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
 92→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
 93→            try {
 94→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
 95→                var line: String?
 96→                val sb = StringBuilder()
 97→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
 98→                    sb.append(line)
 99→                }
100→                rd.close()
101→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
102→            } catch (e: Exception) {
103→                Timber.w(e)
104→            }
105→            return contentOfMyInputStream
106→        }
107→    }
108→}

 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analyticsOptIn"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE.toString())
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }

265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }
278→
279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }

297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.cardviewer
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.content.res.Resources
 22→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 23→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 24→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 25→import com.ichi2.anki.reviewer.ReviewerCustomFonts
 26→import com.ichi2.libanki.Card
 27→import com.ichi2.libanki.Sound
 28→import timber.log.Timber
 29→import java.io.BufferedReader
 30→import java.io.IOException
 31→import java.io.InputStream
 32→import java.io.InputStreamReader
 33→
 34→class HtmlGenerator(
 35→    private val typeAnswer: TypeAnswer,
 36→    val cardAppearance: CardAppearance,
 37→    val cardTemplate: CardTemplate,
 38→    val resources: Resources,
 39→    private val showAudioPlayButtons: Boolean
 40→) {
 41→
 42→    @CheckResult
 43→    fun generateHtml(card: Card, reload: Boolean, side: Side): CardHtml {
 44→        return CardHtml.createInstance(card, reload, side, this)
 45→    }
 46→
 47→    fun filterTypeAnswer(content: String, side: Side): String {
 48→        return when (side) {
 49→            Side.FRONT -> typeAnswer.filterQuestion(content)
 50→            Side.BACK -> typeAnswer.filterAnswer(content)
 51→        }
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    fun expandSounds(content: String): String {
 55→        return if (showAudioPlayButtons) {
 56→            Sound.expandSounds(content)
 57→        } else {
 58→            content
 59→        }
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    companion object {
 63→        fun createInstance(
 64→            context: Context,
 65→            typeAnswer: TypeAnswer
 66→        ): HtmlGenerator {
 67→            val preferences = context.sharedPrefs()
 68→            val cardAppearance = CardAppearance.create(ReviewerCustomFonts(), preferences)
 69→            val cardHtmlTemplate = loadCardTemplate(context)
 70→            val showAudioPlayButtons = preferences.getBoolean(context.getString(R.string.show_audio_play_buttons_key), true)
 71→
 72→            return HtmlGenerator(
 73→                typeAnswer,
 74→                cardAppearance,
 75→                cardHtmlTemplate,
 76→                context.resources,
 77→                showAudioPlayButtons
 78→            )
 79→        }
 80→
 81→        /**
 82→         * Load the template for the card
 83→         */
 84→        private fun loadCardTemplate(viewer: Context): CardTemplate {
 85→            try {
 86→                val data = convertStreamToString(viewer.assets.open("card_template.html"))
 87→                return CardTemplate(data)
 88→            } catch (e: IOException) {
 89→                Timber.w(e)
 90→                throw RuntimeException(e)
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→
 94→        /**
 95→         * Converts an InputStream to a String.
 96→         *
 97→         * @param input InputStream to convert
 98→         * @return String version of the InputStream
 99→         */
100→        private fun convertStreamToString(input: InputStream?): String {
101→            var contentOfMyInputStream = ""
102→            try {
103→                val rd = BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(input), 4096)
104→                var line: String?
105→                val sb = StringBuilder()
106→                while (rd.readLine().also { line = it } != null) {
107→                    sb.append(line)
108→                }
109→                rd.close()
110→                contentOfMyInputStream = sb.toString()
111→            } catch (e: Exception) {
112→                Timber.w(e)
113→            }
114→            return contentOfMyInputStream
115→        }
116→    }
117→}
